rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. KITSIA'S TRANSPORTS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And Quash Service Of Summons And Re-Set Hearing On Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 06, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF BANK OF THE WEST's Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And Quash Service Of Summons And Re-Set Hearing On Motion. Plaintiff Bank of the West's motion to set aside the September 16, 2019 order vacating the default and default judgment against defendants Kitsia's Transports, Inc. and Kitsia Guardado is off calendar without prejudice to being refiled with a proof of service showing that the motion papers were served on Alan Irwin. The September 16, 2019 order shows that Mr. Irwin appeared on behalf of defendants for the hearing on the same date, yet the moving papers on this motion do not show that Mr. Irwin was served with this motion. While Mr. Irwin did not formally substitute in as counsel for defendants, basic fairness, if not due process, requires that Mr. Irwin be served with the moving papers on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HOEM & ASSOCIATES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 06, 2019, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT GEO CELL SOLUTIONS, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-complainant Geo-Cell Solutions, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a second amended cross-complaint is granted on the following specified conditions which Geo-Cell must agree to either orally at the hearing or in writing sent to counsel for cross-defendant Maxxon Corporation prior to the hearing. The conditions that Geo-Cell must agree to are: 1) Geo-Cell must serve verified code-compliant responses without objection other than privilege no later than December 16, 2019 to the following deem-served discovery: a) state all facts in support of all new allegations; b) state the name and contact information for each person who knows any of those facts and what facts each person knows; c) identify all documents which refer to or memorialize such facts and produce them; 2) Geo-Cell must make all persons identified in its responses who are employed by it available for a deposition of no more than three hours in length no later than January 10, 2020; and 3) Maxxon may file a summary adjudication motion directed to the new cross-claims against it to be heard no later than February 21, 2020 on 30 days' notice. If Geo-Cell does not agree to all of these conditions, the motion is denied. Granting the motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions on the granting of the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to Maxxon by the granting of the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Geo-Cell is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, sates that Geo-Cell accepts the conditions, and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 6, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC'S, Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court notes that this hearing is a continued hearing in which the parties have previously stipulated to have JPT McDonald hear the motion and no further stipulation is necessary upon check in with the clerk. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NATHAN DESOMBER VS. SEAN KILLEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Sean Killen To Vacate Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 06, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT SEAN KILLEN's Motion Of Defendant Sean Killen To Vacate Default Judgment. Defendant's motion to vacate the default judgment is granted. Under Code of Civ. Proc. § 473(d) the court may upon motion "set aside any void judgment or order." "[C]ompliance with the statutory procedures for service of process is essential to establish personal jurisdiction. Thus, a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void. Under section 473, subdivision (d), the court may set aside a default judgment which is valid on its face, but void, as a matter of law, due to improper service." (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544, internal citations omitted.) Relief from a judgment that is valid on its face but void for improper service must be sought within 2 years of entry of the default judgment. (Rogers v. Silverman (1989) 216 Cal.App.3d 1114, 1126.) Here, the default judgment was entered on December 19, 2017 and thus Defendant's motion is timely. Defendant declares he was never personally served in this action and that he only became aware of its existence when he "found an envelope containing the summons, complaint, and first amended complaint . . . on [his] doorstep." (Killen decl. ISO Mot. to Vacate, 4-5.) Code of Civ. Proc. § 415.10 provides for personal service as a method of service. "If the complaint and summons were personally delivered to, i.e., handed to, defendant then he could be said to have been personally served." (Am. Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 389.) Alternatively, in lieu of personal service, Code of Civ. Proc. § 415.20 allows for service by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint at the person's dwelling and mailing a copy. Because Defendant declares that the summons and complaint was only left at his home, the judgment is void for improper service. "It is the policy of the law to favor, whenever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . Therefore, when a party in default moves promptly to seek relief, very slight evidence is required to justify a trial court's order setting aside a default. (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal.3d 474, 478.) "When a defendant argues that service of summons did not bring him or her within the trial court's jurisdiction, the plaintiff has the burden of proving the facts that did give the court jurisdiction, that is the facts requisite to an effective service." (Am. Express, 131 Cal.App.4th at 387.) Plaintiff argues that Defendant had actual notice that he was being sued. However, this court finds that insufficient to bring Defendant under the court's jurisdiction. "We question whether such a rationale [of actual notice] can ever sustain a default judgment over a defendant's objection that service did not comply with statutory requirements." (Am. Express, 131 Cal.App.4th at 390-91.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant Sean Killen is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PRINCE M EDMONDS JR VS. LATONYA EVETTE CARPENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion By Plaintiff And Respondent Prince M. Edmonds, Jr. To Consolidate Civil And Probate Cases; Memorandum In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 06, 2019, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PRINCE EDMONDS' Motion To Consolidate Civil And Probate Cases. Plaintiff Prince Edmonds' motion to consolidate is denied. The motion is untimely. The two cases have been pending since February of 2019 and there is no explanation why this motion was not filed earlier. Consolidation will likely cause a continuance of the current trial date in the civil action and the petition to compel accounting in the probate action. While there is some factual overlap between the actions, the court believes that they are sufficiently discrete that consolidation is not necessary to preserve judicial economy, avoid duplicative work, or to prevent inconsistent rulings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant Carpenter is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TYSON QUICK VS. TEN 15, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants Ten 15, Inc. And Elva Patinofranco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 06, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF TYSON QUICK's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Defendants Ten 15, Inc. And Elva Patinofranco. Plaintiff Tyson Quick's motion for terminating sanctions against defendants Ten 15, Inc. and Elva Patinofranco is denied. First, plaintiff does not conclusively demonstrate that defense counsel filed a false declaration on purpose. The misstatement regarding the meet and confer efforts could have been the product of error. (See Stofferahn Dec.) Second, and more importantly, the requested relief far outweighs the alleged transgression. In Slesinger, Inc. v. Walt Disney Co. (2008) 155 Cal.App.4th 736, 740, the court held "that when a plaintiff's deliberate and egregious misconduct makes any sanction other than dismissal inadequate to ensure a fair trial, the trial court has inherent power to impose a terminating sanction." The court explained "that California trial courts have inherent power to issue a terminating sanction when a plaintiff's misconduct is deliberate, is egregious, and makes lesser sanctions inadequate to ensure a fair trial." (Id.) Here, the court believes that a fair trial can go forward even if the meet and confer declaration were falsified. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)
